plainEnglish: This is the stricter Level AAA rule: nothing flashes more than three times in any one second—period, with no 'below threshold' exception based on size or brightness. Avoid all rapid flashing.
whyItMatters: Even small or dim flashing above three times per second can trigger seizures in highly photosensitive users, who get no exception at this level.
quickCheck: Confirm no content—regardless of how small or dim—flashes more than three times in any one-second period.
commonMistakes: Relying on the 2.3.1 'below threshold' allowance, which does not exist at Level AAA.
Small flashing indicators or animated cursors that exceed three flashes per second.
Spinners or loaders that strobe rapidly rather than animate smoothly.

codeExamples.label: Smooth animation, no rapid flashing
Rapid flashing regardless of size
